readme.purpose: Asset model artifact — mu, sigma, correlation, and distribution parameters for Monte Carlo simulation. System file in src/config/, shared by all profiles. v1.0 hand-calibrated baseline; will be superseded by asset_calibration.py pipeline output.
readme.do_not_edit_note: These parameters drive all portfolio return simulations. Changes here affect every profile. Use asset_calibration.py to update from new market data rather than editing manually.
readme.mu_annual: Conservative long-run REAL return estimate (net of inflation). NOT raw historical nominal return and NOT recent-window return. Sources: Vanguard/BlackRock 10yr CMAs, CRSP long-run data. Example: 0.065 = 6.5% real annual return. The simulator adds the inflation rate from inflation_yearly.json on top to get nominal returns.
readme.sigma_annual: Annualized return volatility (standard deviation). Long-run estimate — not a recent-window figure. Drives the spread of Monte Carlo paths.
readme.expense_ratio: Annual fund expense ratio subtracted from returns every year. E.g. 0.0009 = 0.09% (9 basis points). Already reflected in net-of-fee mu.
readme.tracking_error: Additional per-year return noise above the asset class model. 0.0 for broad index funds. 0.03-0.04 for single stocks or active funds.
readme.dist_yield_annual: Annual distribution yield (dividends + interest). Drives taxable income in BROKERAGE accounts each year. Distributions from TRAD and Roth accounts are NOT taxed annually — they are tax-deferred or tax-free respectively.
readme.dist_qualified_ratio: Fraction of annual distributions taxed at LTCG rates (0/15/20%) rather than ordinary income rates. E.g. 0.9 = 90% qualified dividends. 0.0 for Treasury interest (always ordinary income). Only relevant for BROKERAGE accounts.
readme.tax_note: Distributions from BROKERAGE accounts generate taxable income each year: (yield × balance × qualified_ratio) taxed at LTCG rates, remainder at ordinary rates. TRAD IRA distributions are taxed at ordinary rates only when withdrawn. Roth IRA distributions are always tax-free.
readme._provenance: Audit trail per ticker — basis for parameter choices. Ignored by simulator. Keep updated when changing parameters.
readme.auto_refresh_enabled: false — asset model is managed manually or via asset_calibration.py pipeline. Do not run refresh_assets.py against this file.
readme.correlations: Class-level empirical long-run correlation matrix. Conservative estimates — lower than peak-crisis correlations. Used by Monte Carlo to generate correlated return paths across asset classes.
